markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Render missing media/link/embed targets as repairable broken nodes with backend repair actions. This MT belongs to RichDocumentCore. Build the canonical structured document model backing the editor, Loom, UserManual, and projections.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Rich documents are PostgreSQL/EventLedger/CRDT-backed structured block documents, not Markdown vault authority.
All rich document state must preserve Raw/Derived/Display separation and stable entity IDs.
scope.expected_behavior: BrokenEmbedRepairState leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: BrokenEmbedRepairState is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence: Built+verified (commits b506d832 backend core + 935e0482 manual + bf0c62a5 integration tests + dd3056f5 frontend); RichDocumentCore: 6 real-PG integration + 27 unit backend tests + 8 vitest (app suite 349/349). 18 block kinds, RawDerivedDisplay (CX-100), stable KBL- ids, save/load API w/ receipts, projections (negative proof: projection-delete leaves authority byte-identical), embeds (absolute paths DB-CHECK rejected), backlinks (stable KDLNK- relationship_id), history, permissions, crash recovery; migrations 0280-0282; RichDocumentView.tsx wired to real authority with stable selectors for MT-161+ GUI groups. UserManual coverage added (1 group + 10 cmd refs).

findings.title: Broken-embed queue is not reconciled against document content
findings.evidence: list_knowledge_document_broken_embeds (knowledge.rs:4891) reads the side table; nothing scans content_json embed blocks to populate it, so a document can reference a missing media id with an empty broken-embed queue.
findings.fix: Populate/repair the embeds table from content_json on save (see MT-152 fix) so the broken-embed surface reflects real document references.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-153 (BrokenEmbedRepairState) requires rendering missing media/link/embed targets as repairable broken nodes with backend repair actions, PostgreSQL-backed. The code delivers a typed embed model, a real path-rejecting and repair-state-pair CHECK migration, storage methods for the broken/ok transition and broken queue, and HTTP endpoints (list broken queue + repair with receipt), all proven against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL. PASS_V2.
findings: Model: embed.rs:231-288 defines EmbedRepairAction (Relink/Reresolve/Remove) and BrokenEmbedRepair carrying block_id, the failed target, a reason, and available_actions -- the required repairable-node data. Unit test embed.rs:432-446 asserts all 3 actions offered.

Migration: migrations/0281_knowledge_document_embeds.sql creates knowledge_document_embeds with repair_state DEFAULT 'ok' CHECK IN ('ok','broken'), paired CHECK ((repair_state='broken') = (repair_reason IS NOT NULL)), a path-rejecting ref_value CHECK, a partial broken-row index, and schema-registry registration. Real durable PG authority, no SQLite.

Storage: storage/knowledge.rs:5553-5578 set_knowledge_document_embed_repair_state (Some(reason)->broken, None->ok) and :5635-5648 list_knowledge_document_broken_embeds (WHERE repair_state='broken'). Real sqlx against the PG pool.

API: api/knowledge_documents.rs:1291-1312 list_broken_embeds (returns broken_embeds + available_actions) and :1316-1348 repair_embed (POST embeds/:embed_id/repair, permission-gated DocumentAction::Write, emits a KnowledgeRichDocumentSaved receipt). Routes at :95-100.

REAL-PG PROOF (sub-rule 2): tests/knowledge_document_model_tests.rs:271-364 (embed_reference_rejects_absolute_paths_and_broken_embed_is_repairable) runs on Handshake-managed PostgreSQL via knowledge_pg_support.rs (explicit: NO SQLite, in-memory, or mock fallback; managed cluster, isolated schema, real migrations). Inserts an ok embed, marks it broken, asserts the broken queue returns it, repairs to ok, asserts the queue empties; also proves the DB CHECK rejects an absolute-path ref_value.

SUB-RULE 1: no deferred-live escapes; grep found no todo!/unimplemented!/unreachable!/pending-MT/*Unavailable on the live path; the only placeholder hit is a doc-comment describing the editor repairable-placeholder UI node (intended feature).

Spec alignment: current Master Spec v02.194 02-system-architecture.md (block documents with embeds, Raw/Derived/Display separation) is consistent with typed embed refs and broken state as repairable data.

Adversarial-v2 LOW: broken-embed queue is not auto-populated by scanning content_json on save. Hardening refinement, not a fail-level gap: MT-required behavior is fully implemented and real-PG proven, and the repair API can mark any embed broken by id.
Aside (non-blocking, expected): packet cites v02.192/v02.193 vs current v02.194; paperwork drift is not a failing reason.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
